contract.name: v2/autocomplete/cfda.md
contract.description: Returns CFDA program autocomplete suggestions based on a search string with an optional result cap.

search_text.description: Substring used to match CFDA program numbers, titles, or popular names (matching is case-insensitive).
search_text.constraints: Required JSON string with length > 0. Missing, null, or empty strings return 400 (detail: 'Missing one or more required request parameters: search_text'). Non-string primitives (for example numbers) surface HTML 500 errors, while arrays are treated as missing and return the same 400 message. Input is not trimmed; whitespace-only strings are accepted verbatim and yield matches based on the literal spaces.

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return. Defaults to 10 when omitted.
limit.constraints: Optional. Values >= 0 are honored after integer coercion (numeric strings, decimals, booleans). 0 returns an empty result set. Non-numeric strings (including empty string) return 400 with detail 'Limit request parameter is not a valid, positive integer'. Values that coerce to negative integers, null, or arrays trigger HTML 500 errors instead of JSON.

results.description: Array of CFDA matches sorted lexicographically by program_number, capped by the effective limit. Each element is an object with string fields program_number, program_title, and popular_name (popular_name may be an empty string). Empty array indicates no matches.

results.program_title: The Language Flagship Grants to Institutions of Higher Education
Garrett A. Morgan Technology and Transportation Education Program (GAMTTEP)

body.detail: Limit request parameter is not a valid, positive integer

quirks: Negative limits (including numeric strings) and null or array values yield HTML 500 responses instead of structured JSON errors.
search_text is not trimmed; leading or trailing whitespace changes matching and even space-only strings return results.
Boolean, decimal, and numeric-string limit values are coerced to integers (true=1, false=0).
Large limit requests (for example 20000) return the entire CFDA catalog (~3.8k records) in one payload with no pagination metadata.
risks: Clients must guard against unparseable HTML 500 responses by validating limit inputs client-side.
Requesting very large limits can pull the full dataset into memory and impact latency or client parsing.
popular_name often comes through empty or with irregular spacing; callers should tolerate blank display values.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"program_number":"12.330","program_title":"Science, Technology, Engineering & Mathematics (STEM) Education, Outreach and Workforce Program","popular_name":""}, ...]}

probes.notes: Default request returned 10 matches sorted by program_number, confirming the implicit limit of 10.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"program_number":"12.550","program_title":"The Language Flagship Grants to Institutions of Higher Education"}, ...]}

probes.notes: Honors limit=5 and returns both populated and empty popular_name values.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"program_number":"10.001","program_title":"Agricultural Research Basic and Applied Research"}, ... 3767 items]}

probes.notes: Large limit returned the full dataset (~3,767 programs) sorted by program_number.
Negative limit supplied as a string causes an HTML 500 instead of a JSON validation message.
Boolean true is coerced to limit=1.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Missing one or more required request parameters: search_text"}

probes.notes: Array value for search_text is rejected with the same 400 message used for missing input.

mismatches: Docs describe limit as a simple number, but negative, null, or array values produce HTML 500 errors instead of typed validation feedback.
Docs imply invalid search_text types should be handled by schema validation, yet numeric inputs trigger HTML 500 responses.
risks: Clients must guard against unparseable HTML 500 responses by validating limit inputs client-side.
Requesting very large limits can pull the full dataset into memory and impact latency or client parsing.
popular_name often comes through empty or with irregular spacing; callers should tolerate blank display values.
